evals.prompt: Set up Better Auth with Google login for my Next.js app using Drizzle ORM
evals.expectedBehavior: Should ask for confirmation, read better-auth-guide.md and oauth-social-login.md, create auth config, database schema, middleware, and login UI components

evals.prompt: I need authentication for my app. I'm not sure which provider to use.
evals.expectedBehavior: Should trigger auth-setup skill, present decision framework with Better Auth and Firebase Auth only, ask clarifying questions (mobile support? self-hosted?), then guide to appropriate provider. Should NOT offer Clerk, Auth.js, or Supabase Auth as options.

evals.prompt: Add Clerk auth with GitHub and Google OAuth to my existing Next.js project
evals.expectedBehavior: Should inform user that Clerk is not yet supported and suggest Better Auth or Firebase Auth as alternatives. Should NOT proceed with Clerk setup.

evals.prompt: Set up Firebase Auth for my app that needs to support both web and mobile (KMP)
evals.expectedBehavior: Should recommend Firebase Auth (best KMP support), read firebase-auth-guide.md, set up client + admin SDK, session cookies with httpOnly, and warn about PRIVATE_KEY newline issue

evals.prompt: I'm already using Supabase for my database. Add auth with email/password and Google login
evals.expectedBehavior: Should inform user that Supabase Auth is not yet supported. Suggest Better Auth as alternative (can connect to Supabase's PostgreSQL database via Drizzle ORM).

evals.prompt: Set up the simplest possible auth for a prototype app
evals.expectedBehavior: Should suggest Better Auth as the recommended option (self-hosted, zero cost, simple setup). Should NOT suggest Auth.js as it is not currently supported.

evals.prompt: What auth pitfalls should I watch out for before deploying to production?
evals.expectedBehavior: Should read 09-common-pitfalls.md, present relevant pitfalls organized by category (session, OAuth, database, Next.js, security, Better Auth-specific), not trigger full setup flow

evals.prompt: Set up Better Auth with email verification and complete password reset flow for my Next.js app
evals.expectedBehavior: Should read better-auth-guide.md, create auth config with requireEmailVerification: true and sendVerificationEmail/sendResetPassword handlers, create all UI components including reset-password.tsx, set up middleware using cookie-based pattern (NOT auth() wrapper — that's Auth.js, see pitfall #43), and document the full forgot → email → reset → login flow

evals.prompt: I need production-ready Better Auth with rate limiting and security headers for my SaaS app
evals.expectedBehavior: Should read better-auth-guide.md, configure rateLimit (10 req/60s), session management (expiresIn, updateAge, cookieCache), reference next.config.ts security headers template, explain that Better Auth handles CSRF internally, and create the file creation checklist

evals.prompt: My Better Auth forgot-password email sends a link but users get a 404 error when they click it
evals.expectedBehavior: Should identify that reset-password.tsx is missing, provide the component from assets, explain that requestPasswordReset({ redirectTo }) sends users to /reset-password which needs a page, and guide through creating the route at app/(auth)/reset-password/page.tsx

evals.prompt: Set up Firebase Auth for my Next.js app with Google OAuth. I want all the UI components and middleware.
evals.expectedBehavior: Should copy components from assets/components/firebase/ (NOT from root assets/components/), copy middleware from assets/middleware/firebase/nextjs-middleware.ts, and copy shared components (globals.css, dashboard-page.tsx) from assets/components/shared/. Should NOT mix Better Auth and Firebase components.

evals.prompt: I have an existing Next.js project with Tailwind and custom colors (blue-600 primary). I want to add Better Auth without changing my existing design.
evals.expectedBehavior: Should detect existing Tailwind setup (Scenario B), read globals.css and tailwind.config.ts, identify custom colors, merge shadcn/ui CSS variables into existing globals.css mapping blue-600 to --primary, install tailwindcss-animate if missing, then proceed with Better Auth setup. Should NOT overwrite existing globals.css.

evals.prompt: Add Firebase Auth to my project that already uses shadcn/ui with the New York style
evals.expectedBehavior: Should detect shadcn/ui via components.json (Scenario A), SKIP globals.css generation, note the New York style, and proceed directly with Firebase Auth setup using existing theme. Should reference 10-existing-project-integration.md.
